---
title: "Validation: Student's t-test vs. exact permutation test"
date: "Compiled at `r format(Sys.time(), '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', tz = 'UTC')` UTC"
output:
  github_document: default
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
params:
  name: "01_exact_vs_ttest" # change if you rename file
---

```{r here, message=FALSE}
here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".Rmd"), uuid = "88a30f86-0df6-4db8-8217-c59705f33b34")
```

```{r global-options, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  dev = c("png", "cairo_pdf"),
  fig.ext = c("png", "pdf"),
  fig.width = 10, 
  fig.height = 5, 
  dpi = 200,
  fig.path = paste0('figures/', params, "/"),
  message = FALSE, 
  warning = FALSE,
  echo = FALSE)
```

## Objective

We evaluate whether the two-sample Student's t-test yields results sufficiently close to the exact permutation test under ideal conditions (normality, equal variance), to justify its use as a ground truth in simulation studies.

## Design

- Data: Independent, normally distributed with equal variances
- Sample sizes per group: n ∈ {2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12}
- Effect sizes: mu ∈ {0, 1, 3} (difference in means)
- Repetitions: 100

We compute and compare p-values from:
- Student's t-test
- Exact permutation test (all group label permutations)

```{r libraries}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)
library(purrr)
library(furrr)
library(progressr)
library(fs)
```

```{r directories}
# create or *empty* the target directory, used to write this file's data: 
#projthis::proj_create_dir_target(params$name, clean = TRUE)

# function to get path to target directory: path_target("sample.csv")
path_target <- projthis::proj_path_target(params$name)

# function to get path to previous data: path_source("00-import", "sample.csv")
path_source <- projthis::proj_path_source(params$name)
```

## Functions

```{r functions}
t_test <- function(x1, x2) {
  n <- length(x1)
  tstat <- (mean(x1) - mean(x2)) / sqrt(1/n * (var(x1) + var(x2)))
  2 * pt(abs(tstat), df = 2*n-2, lower.tail = FALSE)
}

perm_test <- function(x1, x2) {
  x <- c(x1, x2)
  n <- length(x1)
  combs <- combn(2*n, n)
  tstat_obs <- (mean(x1) - mean(x2)) / sqrt(1/n * (var(x1) + var(x2)))
  tstats_perm <- apply(combs, 2, function(idx) {
    x1_perm <- x[idx]
    x2_perm <- x[-idx]
    (mean(x1_perm) - mean(x2_perm)) / sqrt(1/n * (var(x1_perm) + var(x2_perm)))
  })
  mean(abs(tstats_perm) >= abs(tstat_obs))
}

run_one_setting <- function(i, path_results, reps) {
  setting <- settings[i, ]
  n <- setting$n
  mu <- setting$mu

  result_file <- file.path(path_results, paste0("n-", n, "_mu-", mu, ".Rds"))

  if (!file.exists(result_file)) {
    df <- map_dfr(1:reps, function(r) {
      set.seed(10000 + 100 * n + 10 * mu + r)
      x1 <- rnorm(n, mean = mu)
      x2 <- rnorm(n, mean = 0)
      tibble(
        rep = r,
        p_ttest = t_test(x1, x2),
        p_perm = perm_test(x1, x2),
        n = n,
        mu = mu
      )
    })

    saveRDS(df, result_file)
  }

  invisible(NULL)
}
```

## Setting

```{r}
n_vec <- c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10)
mu_vec <- c(0, 1, 2, 3)
reps <- 100
path_results <- path_target()
dir_create(path_results)

settings <- expand.grid(n = n_vec, mu = mu_vec)
```


## Parallel Simulation

```{r parallel-simulation, eval=FALSE}
handlers("txtprogressbar")

cores <- 10

if (parallel::detectCores() < cores) {
  cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 2
}
plan(multisession, workers = cores)

with_progress({
  pb <- progressor(steps = nrow(settings))
  future_map(1:nrow(settings), ~{
    pb()
    run_one_setting(.x, path_results = path_results, reps = reps)
  })
})

plan(sequential)
```

## Combine results

```{r results}
results <- map_dfr(1:nrow(settings), function(i) {
  n <- settings$n[i]
  mu <- settings$mu[i]
  file <- file.path(path_results, paste0("n-", n, "_mu-", mu, ".Rds"))
  readRDS(file)
})
```

## Type I Error and Power

```{r error-power}
summary_df <- results %>%
  mutate(H0 = mu == 0) %>%
  group_by(n, H0) %>%
  summarize(
    type1_t = mean(p_ttest <= 0.05),
    type1_perm = mean(p_perm <= 0.05),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

kable(summary_df, caption = "Type I error (H0=TRUE) and power (H0=FALSE)")
```

## p-value Comparison Plots

```{r}
axis_text_size <- 14
axis_title_size <- 16
title_size <- 16
strip_size <- 14
```


### Boxplot: Difference in p-values
```{r boxplot-p-diff, fig.width=10, fig.height=5, dpi = 200}
results <- results %>%
  dplyr::filter(mu != 0) %>%
  mutate(p_diff = abs(p_perm - p_ttest))

ggplot(results, aes(x = factor(n), y = p_diff)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, fill = "lightblue") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~mu, labeller = label_bquote(mu == .(mu)), ncol = 1) +
  labs(title = "Absolute difference between permutation and t-test p-values",
       x = "n (per group)",
       y = expression("|" * p[perm] - p[ttest] * "|")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = axis_text_size),
        axis.title = element_text(size = axis_title_size),
        plot.title = element_text(size = title_size),
        strip.text = element_text(size = strip_size)
  )
```


### Scatterplot
```{r scatter}
filtered <- results %>% filter(mu == 2)

ggplot(filtered, aes(x = p_ttest, y = p_perm)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
  facet_wrap(~ n, labeller = label_bquote(n == .(n))) +
  labs(x = "t-test p-value", y = "Permutation test p-value",
       title = expression("Comparison of p-values (" * mu * " = 2)")) +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Conclusion

The p-values from the t-test closely match those from the exact permutation test under the tested settings. Thus, for simulations under normality and equal variance, the t-test can reliably be used as a surrogate for the exact permutation test.
